metadata.title: Why 4s Fills Before 3d but Leaves First

beats.narration_text: Namaste. Liam, in for Bear. Chemistry's Aufbau mnemonic places four s before three d. Yet when iron is ionized, its four-s electrons leave before its three-d electrons. The apparent paradox comes from treating a mnemonic like a fixed energy chart.

props.command: Why does 4s fill before 3d but ionize first in transition metals?
props.segment: Why 4s Fills Before 3d but Leaves First
props.runningText: comparing configurations and many-electron energy differences…

beats.narration_text: Neutral iron's ground configuration is argon core, three d six, four s two. The familiar n-plus-ell order is a useful Aufbau bookkeeping rule, not a claim that one immutable four-s orbital energy stays below three d in every atom and ion.
Remove two electrons and the ground configuration of iron two-plus is argon core, three d six. The four-s occupancy is gone while the three-d count remains six.
How can four s appear earlier in the Aufbau order yet be removed first? Because filling order, orbital labels, and ionization energies are not one fixed list of numbers.
The naive model assigns permanent energies to four s and three d, then assumes lower means both filled first and removed last. Multi-electron atoms do not obey that frozen one-electron diagram.
Electron-electron repulsion, screening, penetration, exchange, and orbital relaxation all matter. Change the occupation or charge state, and the self-consistent orbitals and their energies change.

The Madelung sequence summarizes common neutral-atom filling patterns. It is not a measured universal energy ladder, and it does not say neutral iron's occupied four-s orbital must be lower than every occupied three-d orbital in the final atom.

Ionization compares total energies of different many-electron states, including relaxation after an electron leaves. For iron, those energy differences favor removing the more diffuse four-s electrons before the three-d electrons.
Aufbau asks how to organize a ground-state configuration across atoms. Ionization asks which total-energy difference is smallest for a particular charge state. Related physics, different questions.

If someone writes iron two-plus as argon three d four four s two, check the observed ground configuration. It is argon three d six. Remove four s first, but do not justify that with a frozen orbital-energy cartoon.

Four s before three d is an Aufbau mnemonic. Four-s removal from iron follows many-electron total-energy differences and orbital relaxation. Orbital energies depend on configuration; filling is not ionization run backward.
Your turn. Neutral iron is argon three d six four s two. Remove two four-s electrons. What remains? Argon three d six, the ground configuration of iron two-plus.
Why 4s Fills Before 3d but Leaves First.
